zh.Speldhurst, Kent, England: 英國肯特斯培爾度爾斯特
zh.Parham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom: 英國英格蘭素福爾可帕哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Waller, Sheriff of Kent於1394年出生在英國肯特格盧布利其時，他的父親John Waller29歲，母親Margaret Lansdall20歲。他和Margaret Waller Gulby至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1440年在英國肯特斯培爾度爾斯特享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Waller, Sheriff of Kent于1394年出生在英国肯特格盧布利其时，他的父亲John Waller29岁，母亲Margaret Lansdall20岁。他和Margaret Waller Gulby至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1440年在英国肯特斯培爾度爾斯特享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Waller,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English waler, waliere, walour, waller ‘builder of walls, mason’.
English: in Sussex, perhaps a topographic name for someone who lived by a prominent wall.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a spring, stream, or man-made well, from Middle English waller, a derivative of Middle English walle, Old English wælle, wælle. It is a West Midlands dialect form of Weller .

variants.name: Wallar
meanings: English: variant of   Waller  .

variants.name: Weller
meanings: English (Surrey, Sussex, and Kent): topographic name for someone who lived by a spring, stream, or man-made well, from Middle English weller, willer, an agent derivative of Middle English welle (in southern England) and wille (in south western England), from Old English wella, well(e), wiella, wielle, willa, wyll(a), wylle. This type of name, a topographic term + -er, is particularly typical of Sussex and its neigbouring counties.
English: occupational name for someone who casts metal or who boils salt, from Middle English weller, willer, a derivative with -er of Old English wellan, wiellan ‘to boil’. Compare 5 below.
North German: variant of   Welle  , cognate with 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Sheriff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name or nickname from Middle English shirreve, sher(r)eve, sheref(fe) ‘sheriff, officer of the Crown’ (Old English scīrgerēfa, from scīr ‘shire, county’ + (ge)rēfa ‘reeve’), literally the reeve (i.e. steward) of a shire. Compare Reeve .
Muslim (mainly Nigeria, Sierra Leone, and Liberia): variant of   Sharif  . In Sierra Leone and Liberia this name is found mainly among the Mandinka people. Compare   Sirleaf  .

variants.name: Sirleaf
meanings: West African (Liberia): altered form of the predominantly Mandinka Muslim name   Sheriff  .

variants.name: Reeve
meanings: English (East Anglia): occupational name from Middle English reve, refe ‘reeve’ (Old English gerēfa). In medieval England a reeve was an official responsible for the administration of a manor, including organizing work done by the peasants on the land for their lord, collecting rents, selling produce, and so on.
Irish: variant of   Reeves   5.

variants.name: Shreve
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Sheriff  . Compare   Shreeve  ,   Shreves  .

variants.name: Sherriff
meanings: English: variant of   Sheriff  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Kent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from the county of Kent. The surname is also established in Ireland, where English bearers of this name settled in Meath, in the 13th century. The name has been Gaelicized as Ceannt.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Waller, Sheriff of Kent was born in 1394, in Groombridge, Kent, England, his father, John Waller, was 29 and his mother, Margaret Lansdall, was 20. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Margaret Waller Gulby. He died in 1440, in Speldhurst, Kent, England, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Waller, Sheriff of Kent est né en 1394, à Groombridge, Kent, Angleterre, son père, John Waller, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Margaret Lansdall, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Margaret Waller Gulby. Il est décédé en 1440, à Speldhurst, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Waller, Sheriff of Kent 1394 in Groombridge, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Waller 29 und seine Mutter Margaret Lansdall 20. Er hatte mit Margaret Waller Gulby mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1440 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Speldhurst, Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Waller, Sheriff of Kent è nato il nel 1394 nel Groombridge, Kent, England, il suo padre, John Waller, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Lansdall, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Margaret Waller Gulby. Morì nel 1440, nel Speldhurst, Kent, England, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Waller, Sheriff of Kent(이)가 1394년 영국 켄트 그룸브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Waller(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Lansdall(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Margaret Waller Gulby(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1440년 영국 켄트 스펠드후르스트에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.
